name: jo
long_description:
jo is a personal AI that runs on your Mac and a private cloud machine we host for you. It knows your life, improves itself autonomously, chats via Telegram and WhatsApp, and never shares your data. 

Less "chatbot" and more "a second brain that does things."
one_liner: A new personal AI that actually knows you.

name: Autostep
long_description: Autostep is a desktop app installed across your organization to surface repetitive, high-cost tasks that fly under the radar. We rank them by cost and impact so you know what's worth automating, then build or find the right fix - whether that's an AI agent, a process change, or an existing tool.
one_liner: Uncover repetitive tasks and turn them to agents

name: Humwork
long_description: Humwork lets AI agents chat with human experts in real-time. When AI agents like Claude Code or OpenClaw hit a wall, Humwork's MCP server connects them to a verified domain expert in under 30 seconds. Our experts include senior engineers, lawyers, marketers, designers, and more.
one_liner: Connect AI agents to human experts in 30 seconds

name: Miso Labs
long_description: Miso Labs is building the world’s most emotive foundation models for voice. We believe that the next generation of AI interactions shouldn't just be functional—they should be human. By bringing warmth and lightning-fast speed to the voice layer, we empower developers to build voice agents that users truly love.
one_liner: The most emotive foundation models for voice

name: Foresight
long_description: Foresight builds AI-powered simulations of human behavior to help companies predict how people will think, react, and choose before making real-world decisions. It lets organizations test countless possible moves in advance, see how each one is likely to play out, and choose the strongest path forward.
one_liner: On-demand consumer insights

name: Smartbase
long_description: Smartbase is an AI-native ERP for manufacturers. We have traditional ERP features like inventory management, and we use AI to automate office work like quoting and order entry. In the past six weeks, we’ve grown from $0 to $171k in contracted ARR.
one_liner: AI-native ERP for manufacturers

name: FinalDose
long_description: FinalDose is building the first programmable drug - a single smart drug molecule that you can insert any diseased genetic code and it can search and destroy cells upon DNA recognition.  Unlocking 80% of undruggable targets today. Starting with all cancers.
one_liner: Programmable DNA drug destroying all cancers, unlocking 80% of targets

name: Prism
long_description: Prism is an AI-native recruiting agency. We use software and agents to find companies better people faster.
one_liner: AI-native recruiting agency

name: TakeCareOS
long_description: TakeCareOS is building the infrastructure for the AI-native long term care providers. Today’s care providers are held back by massive administrative overhead, buried under fragmented tools and manual workflows. TakeCareOS consolidates the entire care lifecycle—scheduling, billing, documentation, compliance, customer support etc.—into a single, intelligent system driven by AI agents. We are eliminating the operational friction of care coordination to establish a new standard of high-efficiency, highly scalable care practices.
one_liner: AI-native operating system for long term care providers

name: Advanced Metal Research
long_description:
America does not just need more welders. It needs welding knowledge that can scale.

The American Welding Society projects 320,500 new welding professionals will be needed by 2029, with about 80,000 jobs to be filled annually from 2025 to 2029. More than 157,000 current welding professionals are approaching retirement. Welders are only a small share of the skilled-trades workforce, but welding accounts for an outsized share of projected capability demand. 

The market calls it a labor shortage. AMR sees a learning bottleneck.

We are building American-made robotic welding cells that combine computer vision to provide real-time seam tracking and post-weld inspection to turn welding from a scarce manual process into a closed-loop manufacturing system that learns from every weld as it happens.
one_liner: Machine intelligence for American Welding

name: Clara
long_description:
Clara is an AI primary care and longevity practice from the team that founded and grew Circle Medical to $100M in revenue.

Our mission at Clara is to add one billion years of healthspan to humanity. We do this with an AI with deep clinical knowledge, coupled with the ability to sift through medical data to detect trends and patterns that could be missed.

Unlike Claude or ChatGPT, Clara can see your medical data and then act. Chat with Clara as you would a doctor and Clara can help with you make sense of symptoms, get medication refills, order and understand labs, and achieve health goals. Medical recommendations are preliminary until one of our human medical providers in the loop reviews and approves them.
one_liner: AI primary care doctor

name: Imperfect
long_description: The Imperfect app pulls your data to create a personalized AI coach for your next race. It adapts your training, recovery, and nutrition to how your body is actually responding.
one_liner: AI coach for athletes with real lives and epic goals

name: Fuchsia
long_description: Fuchsia handles hardware certification and testing so great products can get to markets faster. AI agents keep our work accurate, timely, and fully traceable for every stakeholder.
one_liner: Vanta for hardware

name: Cignara
long_description:
Companies lose revenue every time a customer is stuck in IVR, waits too long for help, gets blocked by a dumb chatbot, or receives a generic answer from an agent without the right context. These are not just support problems. A complaint is a retention moment, an inquiry is a sales moment, and a support call is often an upsell moment.

We built Cignara to fix this. Cignara provides end-to-end AI voice and chat agents that use customer context, company data, business logic, and policy rules to take personalized, agentic actions. Every response is grounded, every action is policy-bound, governed, and verifiable, and every conversation is tracked as a business outcome.

Fortune 500 brands like LG use Cignara as fully autonomous agents and as real-time assistants for human teams.
one_liner: AI Agents for Fortune 500 grade customer support

name: Drafted
long_description:
Drafted is building multimodal generative models for residential architecture and spatial design.

The product allows users to design homes by combining sketches and direct spatial input to generate coherent floor plans and elevations that can be explored in both 2D and 3D. Users can define footprints, lot boundaries, room placement, relationships, room programs, and square footage constraints that persist across generations and iterative design exploration, then export CAD, PDF, and other files into the rest of the home design and construction process.

More info on the company:
⁃ In the past month, we've had 120k users come to the site, generating 325k+ floor plans through socials and word-of-mouth. 
⁃ A team of 9, made up of researchers from Stanford, Autodesk, Brown, Adobe, and Architects from WeWork, Atmos, and design families
⁃ Raised $17.5M from investors such as Buckley Ventures (Josh Buckley), YC (Aaron Epstein), Patrick Collison, Evan Moore, Convective Capital (Bill Clerico), Ben Silbermann, Jack Altman, Samsung, Starship Ventures, Charlie Songhurst, Ryan Tedder, Moses Moody, Alex Blania, and Kevin Mahaffey
one_liner: Design your home instantly with AI

name: 9 Mothers
long_description:
We are making AI mission systems for the modern battlefield.

Our first product - EDDA - is a small, low power, low cost fully autonomous-capable counter drone system. It's designed to be used on vehicles, bases, or carried - to stop group 1 suicide drones. We have multiple contracts, sales, etc.

We have 3 more products with demand in various stages of development.

More details at https://9mothers.com
one_liner: AI mission systems

name: Sazabi
long_description: The AI-native observability platform for fast-moving engineering teams. Backed by tastemakers from the world's top AI companies: Vercel, Graphite, Daytona, Browserbase, LangChain, Mastra, Replit, and more.
one_liner: The AI-native observability platform for fast-moving engineering…

name: Netter
long_description:
Netter (YC P26) helps companies regain control over their data, so teams can pilot their activities with high precision.

Most companies are sitting on years of valuable data, spread across different formats and disconnected tools that don't talk to each other.

Netter brings technical capacity to companies that don't have an army of data engineers, and tackles their most complex data challenges.

- A nursing home group was chasing unpaid invoices manually across disconnected tools. Netter centralized the collection workflows, automated reminders, real-time visibility, task routing.
- A retail chain had years of loyalty card data sitting unused. Netter cross-referenced it with public demographic data to build precise customer segments and trigger the right promotions to the right people.
one_liner: AI native Palantir for mid market.

name: Superset
long_description: Superset helps engineers run 100s of coding agents in parallel.
one_liner: The IDE for the AI Agents Era

name: Inth
long_description: Inth makes privacy compliance live where the risk actually is: the codebase and runtime. Companies still rely on dashboards, questionnaires, and policy docs to prove compliance, but consent breaks in code, deletion breaks in code, and vendors, logs, AI systems, and agent-written changes all move user data in code. Inth sits in the repo, detects user-data privacy risk, maps it to files and owners, and generates the evidence regulators and enterprise buyers ask for. Its wedge is c15t, an open-source consent SDK with 2.6M npm downloads and adoption from teams like Zed, Infisical, and Expo. Inth is making every codebase compliant by default.
one_liner: Privacy compliance, enforced in code

name: Regbase
one_liner: The source of truth for global and local regulation

name: Bloom
long_description:
Bloom is the brand layer for agents - it turns brand into infrastructure that any agent, app, or platform can call.

Ingest your brand from anywhere - decks, websites, social, Figma, and more.
Bloom systemises your brand, enabling the creation of on-brand outputs like images, video, video, copy, and more, through the web app, the API, or MCP.

We're building brand-as-infrastructure for the AI era.
one_liner: Brand infrastructure for AI

name: Dispatch
long_description:
Dispatch is building refurbishable reentry vehicles that host and return payloads for companies making ultra-high-value materials (semiconductors, biotech, and pharma) that can only be manufactured in space. Right now, these companies have no practical way to manufacture and get their products back to Earth. We solve this bottleneck.

We just returned from the Mojave Desert testing our first full-scale reentry heat shield designed to protect the satellite as it returns from space at Mach 20+, built in an apartment for 100x cheaper than competitors. To test, we held it behind a rocket engine to simulate the forces of atmospheric reentry. IT WORKED! Watch here - https://youtu.be/dhlQmnIoptg

To scale, we anchor permanent infrastructure in orbit - building high power Autonomous Space Stations designed from day one for "lights-out" manufacturing serviced by the reentry vehicles.
one_liner: Satellites for manufacturing in space

name: Hedge
long_description: Hedge is an AI-Native specialty insurance company. We help insurance brokers find faster, cheaper, and better coverage for their customers.
one_liner: AI-Native specialty insurance company

name: Surtr Defense Systems
long_description: Surtr builds ParallaxOS, the open operating system for counter-UAS. It takes sensor data from any radar, RF detector, camera, or acoustic array and fuses it into a single unified picture with AI-driven threat classification and engagement recommendations. One interface, any hardware, human in the loop.
one_liner: The open operating system for counter-UAS

name: Klarify
long_description:
Klarify handles every job a therapist does other than the therapy itself, from clinical notes and treatment plans to insurance claims and finding new clients.

Therapists hold the world together. They also run businesses, file insurance claims, write letters, fill forms, supervise new grads, and do hours of paperwork every night. 

Practice management software has existed for decades. It records what therapists do. It can't actually do any of it.

Klara is the AI at the heart of Klarify. She handles notes, treatment plans, clinical letters, insurance claims, between-session resources, supervision prep, and finding new clients.

Nearly 6,000 therapists use Klarify today.

Our mission is to empower therapists, not replace them. So they can focus on the one thing only a human can do. Be present with their client.
one_liner: AI Agent for Therapists

name: Korso
long_description:
Korso builds AI agents that handle the operational work manufacturers still do manually, including processing RFQs, following up on quotes, coordinating with suppliers, and keeping production on track when things go wrong. Our agents plug into your existing ERP and CRM, understand your workflows, and act on your behalf: triaging incoming requests, chasing delayed orders, notifying customers, and escalating when human judgment is needed.

We built Korso around the reality that enterprise systems cannot afford mistakes. Every agent action passes through verification layers, tools are scoped, and critical operations go through dry-run validation and secondary review. Full audit trails mean you can see exactly what was done, why, and when.

Most AI automation breaks down at the ERP integration layer. Korso's orchestration is built for the messiness of real manufacturing systems, with long-running workflows that span weeks and checkpointing that never loses progress.

Instead of replacing your systems, Korso makes them work harder, so your team spends less time on coordination and more time on the work that actually matters.
one_liner: The Intelligence Layer for Manufacturing.

name: primitive
long_description: Building the communication infrastructure needed for fully autonomous agents
one_liner: Communication for agents

name: Standard Signal
long_description: Standard Signal is the first hedge fund that researches and executes trades purely with AI. Our custom models discover and trade on new fundamental truths about the world before humans can.
one_liner: Hedge fund where AI researches and executes every trade

name: Stage
long_description:
Stage is a code review platform designed to help engineers better understand and review AI generated code.

Engineering teams are shipping faster than ever with coding agents, but now they’re drowning in an endless backlog of PRs to review. Stage reorganizes the code review process around how engineers naturally read changes, enabling your team to review (and merge!) PRs 5x faster than GitHub.
one_liner: Code review platform for humans and agents

name: PerfectBit
long_description: We create a new kind of data for training AI models. Most LLMs are pre-trained on noisy web-scraped text, but they hallucinate and still fail on tasks that humans find trivial.  PerfectBit creates high-quality training data that's correct by construction. We verify against physics simulators, scientific databases, formal proof systems. LLMs, robotics, AI for Science, and more.
one_liner: Training data for frontier AI labs

name: Memoir
long_description:
AI marketing service for developer tools. We turn your real product into social posts and demo videos in your voice automatically. Think of it as a clone of you that's also marketing your company at the same time.

Memoir is one system. It reads your repo, drives your live staging app with an AI agent, generates branded demo videos with synthetic data so dashboards look real and narrates them in a voice cloned from yours. For the social side, we monitor what's actually getting traction in your space right now. The threads going viral on dev-tools X, the posts climbing on HN, the formats your audience is engaging with this week, and we draft posts in your writing voice that ride those currents. You review everything before it goes out. The output is indistinguishable from how you'd write and record it yourself, if you had the time and a full marketing team behind you.
one_liner: AI CMO for Software Companies

name: WithAI
long_description:
WithAI helps institutional investors research stocks and manage their portfolios. We're live with four hedge funds, and our users spend 5+ hours a day in the platform.

Investors who try "nearly every tool on the market," to quote a client of ours, "wind up with an unwieldy collection of fragmented point solutions." Those who focus on building a great setup with general agents are instead "signing up for a full-time job." So we deploy a platform called Multiplier that unifies structured data, unstructured data, AI work, human work, and tacit firm knowledge in one secure, governed, customized application.

Our first vertical: independent equity funds with $250M-$5B of assets. They know AI is essential for staying relevant, but they don't have the time or talent to figure out what to do about it. But once we deploy Multiplier––"We used to spend 80% of our time gathering information and 20% of our time acting on it," said one customer. "WithAI has flipped those percentages."
one_liner: Agent harnesses for asset managers

name: Totalis
one_liner: derivative layer for prediction markets

name: Replicas
long_description:
Replicas is a background coding agent that allows you to delegate tasks to Claude Code or Codex from Slack, Linear, GitHub, and more. Each task works inside of a sandboxed virtual machine, allowing teams to heavily parallelize work and burn through tickets.

Replicas agents output higher quality PRs via feedback loops - each task automatically is notified and fixes CI failures and reads all code reviews. With Environments, engineering teams can configure agents to run their entire codebase locally, verify its work, and provide preview URLs.
one_liner: Run any coding agent harness in the cloud

name: Rudus
long_description: Rudus is an AI-powered takeoff and estimation platform built for concrete contractors. We cut takeoff time by 70% and help estimators bid 3-5× more projects without adding headcount. From plan upload to bid package, every part of the workflow gets faster.
one_liner: AI Takeoffs for Concrete

name: Hexa
long_description: Hexa helps industrial distributors automate their sales and procurement workflows to quote faster and win more bids.
one_liner: Autonomous operations for industrial distributors

name: Scope
long_description:
Scope is the system companies use to measure and improve how agents discover, interact and use their product.

As more products gets used through AI agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and similar agents, agents are starting to influence which tools get chosen, how they get set up, and whether they keep getting used. Most companies still cannot see that process clearly.

We run real workflows across agents and show teams when the agent picks them versus a competitor, where it breaks, where docs or product surfaces confuse the agent, and what to change to get better results and a better agent experience.

I started Scope after working on interpretability research for closed-source models at Princeton and later as an ML engineer in GEO/AEO. I kept seeing the same pattern: these systems were shaping real product discovery and usage, but companies had very little visibility into what the model was actually doing.

We are starting with products that agents can directly interact with, especially APIs, infra products, CLIs, and MCP servers.
one_liner: The infra layer for AI agent experience.

name: Twolabs
long_description:
Building socially intelligent robots for caregiving, to assist the elderly.

Our semi-humanoids go into nursing homes, senior living communities, and care facilities to support elderly residents, handling everyday tasks like feeding, dressing, medication, and object retrieval, while providing genuine social and emotional companionship. We build the full stack in-house: the robot hardware and all the AI that makes it socially capable, so one caregiver can do more for more residents in a field facing chronic staffing shortages.

While the rest of robotics is working on object manipulation like moving boxes and folding laundry, we're focused on what we think is the most meaningful problem: how people and robots can work together.
one_liner: Humanoid robots for caregiving.

name: Lightsprint
long_description:
Lightsprint is a collaborative platform for software teams to plan visually, preview live and ship with an army of cloud agents. Your whole team, including non-engineers, can now ship end to end reliably.

The company is founded by Ben Ong, an AI investor who previously scaled AI products at SEA Group (NYSE: SE) and Temasek ($300B fund), Benedict Chan who was VP Engineering at Chainlink and CTO at BitGo (NYSE:BTGO), and Heng Hong Lee who built Facebook Messenger in 2013 and was Director of Engineering at Fazz (YC S17).
one_liner: Collaborative product development platform

name: matforge
long_description:
We build AI scientists that discover new materials for the semiconductor industry - specifically datacenters and fabs. Finding novel materials today takes 10+ years of lab work. We aim to compress that timeline to months, using a swarm of AI agents.

Akash completed his PhD at Stanford on material discovery for semiconductors. The materials he discovered for nanoscale interconnects have been adopted into the roadmaps of Intel and TSMC. Advaith was the founding applied scientist at Persona AI (acquired by Luma Labs), where he built long horizon autonomous agents for major telecom and crypto companies.

Power consumption and heat release from AI chips is doubling every year. The semiconductor industry needs new materials to keep this exponential going - Matforge will help find them.
one_liner: AI scientists to discover materials for the semiconductor industry.

name: Saffron
long_description:
Saffron allows companies to quickly evaluate how well software engineers use AI to code. 

The way engineers write code has fundamentally changed, but the way companies evaluate them hasn't. Saffron gives teams full visibility into how candidates use AI agents to build, debug, and ship real features on an actual codebase. 

Saffron was founded by Robert Chondro (formerly MIT and Jane Street), Jerry Yao (Stanford and Jane Street), and Kazuma Choji (Harvey Mudd, published in NeurIPS and ICML), and is backed by Y Combinator (P26) and Afore Capital.
one_liner: Helping your company find 10x engineers.

name: Chert
long_description: Chert provides the infrastructure for businesses to automate conversations over iMessage. We’re already working with B2C startups, customer experience teams, and sales teams to reach end users through trusted and conversational messaging.
one_liner: iMessage infrastructure for trusted communication at scale

name: Incandor
long_description: Incandor detects fraud on banking and fintech platforms by learning how every user physically behaves — mouse dynamics, keystroke timing, scroll patterns, and on mobile, how they hold their phone. Founded by two Stanford engineers and backed by Y Combinator, Incandor builds a behavioral map of every user on your platform — no fraud labels or historical data required. At the individual level, it identifies account takeovers with >99% accuracy. At the population level, coordinated rings, mule operators, and coerced sessions separate out naturally. Rather than a black-box risk score, fraud teams query the map via a programmable API.
one_liner: Behavioral intelligence infrastructure for anti-fraud

name: Nine Fives
one_liner: Simple, Networked, Driverless, RF Test Equipment

name: Andustry
long_description: We are an AI-native broker, solving sourcing of machines and intermediate goods. We take over finding suppliers and getting the best quotes for our customers (based on price, quality, lead time, etc.). Our customers have limited resources for procurement and we help them reduce purchase prices by 30% on average.
one_liner: We help manufacturers find suppliers for industrial goods.

name: Zenbu
long_description: Zenbu is an extensible IDE for orchestrating coding agents. Shape it around exactly how you and your team work.
one_liner: The extensible IDE for coding agents

name: InLoop Robotics
long_description:
We are building AI-native fulfillment powered by robotics.
Warehouses without human labor that get better, faster, and cheaper with every order.

Most robotics companies wait until their system is perfect before deploying. We don't. We ship imperfect policies into real warehouses, detect failures in real time, recover automatically when we can, and bring in a remote human operator when we can't.

Every failure becomes training data. Every intervention makes the system better. The result is a fulfillment system that continuously learns from production.
one_liner: Staff your warehouse with robotic employees today

name: Dayjob
long_description: Dayjob does AI Scheduling for Short Haul Trucks. Our scheduling agent plugs into existing ERPs and continuously re-optimises routes in real time - handling new jobs, driver changes, and exceptions automatically. Customers in waste management see 8%+ efficiency gains from day one.
one_liner: AI Scheduling for Short Haul Trucks

name: The Company Company
long_description: The Company Company builds the agent and the infrastructure to run companies.
one_liner: The last agent your company will ever need.

name: Trellis
long_description:
Trellis is building toward a new hospitality platform: one that does not just help guests find homes, but actually operates them with agents. 

Our first wedge is short-term rental property managers, replacing their fragmented stack across guest messages, tasks, cleaners, maintenance, vendors, and owner updates with agents that run operations end-to-end. 

In two months, we’ve grown to $500k ARR across 35 customers and 3000 listings, growing around 15% week over week, with one customer already running day-to-day operations with no human involved.
one_liner: The platform where short-term rentals run themselves

name: Atrisa (formerly Refortifai)
long_description: AI Agents to build, debug and automate analog circuit design
one_liner: AI Agents for Analog Circuit Design

name: Tenet Industries
long_description:
Tenet Industries is building low-cost, mass-manufacturable strike drones. 

We have developed our own HW and SW platform optimized for automated manufacturing, enabling hyperscaling of production across multiple defense categories (FPV, fixed-wing, shahed-type, missiles).

We're using design-for-manufacturing and first principles to reimagine how a defense product is developed and manufactured. 

Our first product is a kamikaze quadcopter.
one_liner: Low-cost, mass-produced strike drones

name: Hub.xyz
long_description: Hub.xyz provides real-world training data to frontier AI labs
one_liner: The API for real-world training data.

name: Light Anchor
long_description:
Light Anchor (YC P26) builds AI-run consumer brands that run at the speed of compute. 

Consumer brands have always been limited by the speed of humans: hiring, coordinating, and sleeping. AI agents that now run operations end-to-end break all those constraints, making a new kind of consumer business possible: one capped by compute, not headcount.

Today, Light Anchor operates one flagship brand and multiple experiment stores, all run by AI agents on a shared platform that compounds with every new brand launched. The vision is thousands of consumer businesses, operating with little to no human intervention.
one_liner: AI-run consumer brands

name: Limrun
long_description:
Coding agents in the cloud don't work for majority of teams who require native capabilities; they lack XCode for iOS, Emulator for Android, Unity for game development teams. We are on a mission to cloudify every local development utility so that regardless of where it runs, your agent can compose an ideal set of capabilities on the fly so you don't need to run it on your laptop.

Today, we provide remote services such as XCode, iOS & Android simulators to enable coding agents running in any sandbox to be able to build and iterate over mobile apps, too.

Replit, Rork, Momentic, Minitap and more agent companies have built mobile development and testing experiences on Limrun platform. Coconote (Quizlet), SorceJobs (W25) and more companies with mobile apps use Limrun with their cloud agents, merging PRs without having to check out the code locally.
one_liner: Extend your cloud agent with XCode, Android and more

name: Superlog
long_description:
Sentry and Datadog dump a stream of alerts. Most are duplicates, most lack context, and you still have to solve the issue yourself.

Superlog is AI-native observability that's meant not to be opened. A wizard scans your repo and installs proper OpenTelemetry instrumentation, then runs daily to keep up as you ship new code. When something breaks, Superlog groups the errors into a single incident, investigates with full context (logs, traces, recent deploys, past Slack threads), and posts one mergeable PR in Slack. You merge it, ignore it, or open it as a Claude Code session and modify it.

Telemetry is vendor-neutral, so you keep every log, trace, and metric we install, even if you leave.
one_liner: Observability that installs itself and fixes the bugs it finds.

name: GovGuard
long_description:
GovGuard is building AI agents that automate government workflows, starting with FOIA requests.

Today, public records teams still spend hours searching for responsive records and applying redactions. Many offices still redact sensitive information line by line with a sharpie. Local governments spend billions processing these requests, and if a city misses a legal deadline, it can be sued for failing to comply with public records law.
one_liner: Automating Government Workflows, starting with FOIA

name: Kinro
long_description:
Kinro helps companies sell insurance through AI assistants such as ChatGPT. We build AI sales agents that manage the end-to-end insurance sales process, from answering questions, personalized coverage explanations and quoting. These agents are continuously evaluated and improved through our proprietary platform, optimizing sales performance while remaining 100% compliant.

Selling insurance requires strict regulatory compliance, deep carrier integrations, and extremely reliable answers. Platforms like OpenAI and Google remain general-purpose and are less likely to build the vertical infrastructure required for regulated industries.

Today, Kinro serves brokers and direct-to-consumer carriers across the US and Europe. Insurance distribution is huge: insurers pay roughly $100B+ in commissions annually, and distributors spend $10B+ every year on advertising to acquire customers.

Pierre-Alexandre led research improving Gemini for financial services at DeepMind. Parth led infrastructure for training and inference at Zoox. Corentin was the first employee in an insurance startup and worked closely with many insurance leaders.
one_liner: AI sales agents for insurance. Sell everywhere your buyers are.

name: Juno
long_description:
Juno is an AI personal health assistant for the 1B+ people living with chronic illness.

Launched in October, Juno now supports 90,000+ patients globally and has grown to $70K MRR.
one_liner: AI Health Assistant for Chronic Illness

name: Zolvo
long_description: Commercial lenders manage billions in assets but service their loans like it's 2005: spreadsheets, legacy software, and teams of people doing manual reconciliation all day. Zolvo replaces that entire back office with AI. We automate reconciliation, verification, collections, and reporting so lenders can scale their portfolios without scaling their teams. We're building the operating system for commercial lending
one_liner: AI that automates servicing for commercial lenders

name: Interfaze
long_description:
Interfaze is an AI model built on a new architecture that merges specialized DNN/CNN models with LLMs for developer tasks that require deterministic output and high consistency like OCR, Object detection, classification, Speech-to-text (STT) and more.

Interfaze has outperforms large labs across 9 frontier benchmarks like OCRBench V2, RedCOCO and more.

Full benchmarks: https://interfaze.ai/leaderboards
Read paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.04101
one_liner: AI model built for deterministic developer tasks like OCR

name: Elyra
long_description:
Elyra is an AI reservation system for restaurants. Instead of missing 40% of incoming phone calls and taking days to reply to emails, leaving over $100,000 in bookings on the table every year, restaurants get AI agents that answer every call and email instantly, 24/7, connected to a reservation system that optimizes table placements in real time so empty seats get filled with the right guests.

Felix and Alan met in primary school in Stockholm and have been building things together ever since.

We launched with a single restaurant and within weeks they hit record occupancy, bookings they used to miss were suddenly being captured automatically, and empty Tuesday nights started filling up. Today we power top restaurants groups. 

There are over 1 million restaurants in the US and Europe alone. The average restaurant loses $100K+ a year to missed calls and slow email replies. At our current pricing, that's a $10B+ market opportunity, and we're the only system that handles the call, the email, and the table allocation in one place.
one_liner: AI Reservation System for Restaurants

name: Wealor
one_liner: AI-native platform for wealth managers

name: HEVN, inc
long_description: HEVN is a B2B banking platform for global payments, built on stablecoin rails and a new API for virtual bank accounts under DASP regulation. Companies use HEVN for global payroll and payment automation, with 70% of transaction volume processed through our API. We are up to 30% cheaper than Wise, while transfers are sent from bank accounts in the company's name and transaction limits of up to $1M per transfer.
one_liner: b2b banking for global payments

name: KelAI
long_description:
Founded by former WorldQuant portfolio manager Jeremie Cohen, KelAI is building the autonomous alpha engine for hedge funds, traders and institutional investors. 

Investment teams now have more data, tools and compute than ever, but research still runs across siloed systems and inefficient workflows. KelAI turns that fragmented process into a compounding AI-native engine that continuously finds, tests and validates trading signals at a pace no human team can match.
one_liner: The AI Quant: Autonomous Alpha Engine for Funds and Traders.

name: Deep Interactions
long_description: Deep Interactions is a humans-in-the-loop AI builder. We don't leave you with a spaghetti mess of a code. We bring product intelligence and power the context layer that enables your team to build in sync - saving companies tokens, time, and nerves. You give us a problem and we ship a working AI product in the same afternoon.
one_liner: Collaborative AI app builder for teams

name: qomplement
long_description:
qomplement is the agentic ERP for retail and logistics companies.

Retail and logistics are the industries with the highest volume of transactions in the world. POs, freight invoices, inventory updates, and shipment confirmations. Every single one still requires a person to process it manually. Their only solution has been hiring more people.

Legacy ERPs were built for humans. qomplement is the agentic ERP that cuts 95% of manual work and handles 100x more transactions with the same headcount. We give procurement and operations teams superpowers: automating purchase order intake, vendor onboarding, item master updates, freight order processing, carrier invoicing, three-way matching, and inventory reconciliation.

Already trusted by some of the largest grocery chains and trucking companies in the world. Headquartered in San Francisco.
one_liner: The Agentic ERP for supply chain operations

name: Pavoot
long_description:
Pavoot is an AI event manager for companies that run in person customer events.

More and more companies are investing in dinners, summits, and community events because relationships drive revenue. But today, running events means sourcing attendees, sending invites, hosting the event, and then manually digging through notes, emails, and spreadsheets afterward to figure out who attended, what was discussed, who is a strong lead, and who should be followed up with. Most teams either send generic "thanks for coming" emails or never properly capture the value created during the event.

Pavoot replaces this fragmented workflow with one AI system that keeps every attendee, interaction, conversation, and follow up connected in one place, making relationship driven growth scalable.

Gohar and Ana met while studying Computer Science at ETH Zürich. Gohar was part of Switzerland’s national chess team at the age of 16 and is a 6x Swiss chess champion. Ana won more than 20 medals in Scientific Olympiads and built internal AI systems used by hundreds of data scientists at some of the largest banks in Latin America.

===

Book a demo: pavoot.com
one_liner: AI Event Manager for Customer Events

name: Madrone
long_description: Madrone builds cooling systems for data centers. In Texas, where most new sites are, Madrone can cool using 30% less power and water, thanks to novel dew-point cooling technology.
one_liner: Maximizing compute via hyper-efficient cooling

name: shotwell.ai
long_description: Shotwell provides dense annotations and quality checks for robotics training data. Data quality is the biggest lever in improving robot model performance, and we have developed models that can automatically produce these annotations and operate at a fraction of the cost and instant turnaround time.
one_liner: Automated Scale AI for robotics data

name: Standout
long_description:
Standout gives every talent and company an autonomous agent that represents them, assess the other side, and makes intros when it matters.

Why it matters? Because this is hiring today:
- Talent use AI to apply to 100s of jobs - this is spam.
- Founders/Recruiting send 100s of AI-generated messages to hire the same talent - this is spam.

This has to stop.

Talent stays fully anonymous until they accept an intro. The best way for passively exploring the market and not missing a life changing opportunity.

In 1 month, 10k talent / 60 companies / 100 intros.
one_liner: Agentic hiring marketplace

name: transload
long_description:
transload helps logistics companies measure shipments using the security cameras they already have. A pallet, a couch, a tire – we capture its true size as it moves through the warehouse.

For our customers, 15% of shipments turn out to be bigger than what the sender claimed. Catching those differences is worth ~$50k per site per month in rebillings and improved trailer utilization.
one_liner: Measure freight items with security cameras

name: Userlens
long_description:
Most B2B churn is preventable - most teams just see it too late. 

By the time a CSM notices, the champion has already quietly disengaged, key features went unadopted, and the renewal conversation is damage control. The dangerous part? The customer might still be logging in, but they've stopped using what actually matters. 

Userlens catches all of that months earlier.

Our AI agents monitor every user at every account the way your best CSM watches your top five strategic accounts:
- feature adoption
- usage trends
- champion engagement
- sentiment shifts in Slack and Intercom
- open support issues. 

When a signal hits (churn risk, renewal, expansion opportunity), Userlens doesn't just alert you. It builds the talking points and deck on top of your team's approved templates and playbook. If you have CSMs, Userlens makes every one of them your best. If you don't have one yet, Userlens runs your book like the best CSM money could buy.
one_liner: First ever Agentic CSMs to upsell & renew 6+ figure deals.

name: Sherpa
long_description: Sherpa automatically increases website conversion rates by analyzing visitor behavior, building new page variants, and continuously A/B testing them. Customers typically see a 15-20% lift on their primary conversion metric in their first 30 days.
one_liner: Turn more website visitors into customers

name: Salesgraph
long_description:
Salesgraph equips reps with the most relevant context throughout the deal stage, providing proactive pre-call notes and post-call next steps. Sellers are CEOs of their deals; they need to know who the buyer is, what triggered them to take the first call, what their pain points are, how much value this new tool is bringing, are there any technical blockers, etc. 

A sale is only good when it doesn't feel like a sale to your customer, but rather the sales rep solved a problem for the customer. This was the problem Ricardo and I solved at Mintlify. We created a business case generator that took in call recordings, background news triggers and job updates, Mintlify's ROI and Pricing calculator, past deals won, and customer case studies --> produced a 3 page document expressing the value Mintlify provided + why the quote presented is justifiable. In doing so we closed more enterprise deals at a higher ACV as our champion was able to build a better purchasing case internally. 

Our goal is to empower every sales team to close deals at a higher ACV, while making sure reps learn from their mistakes and create a smooth sales experience for their end customer.
one_liner: the context layer for fast moving sales teams

name: InstaAgent
long_description:
InstaAgent helps consumer brands scale a single campaign across hundreds of specific audiences on Meta and TikTok, each with its own content, voice, and targeting. Traction: $1M ARR in 10 months.

From one brief, we create personalized creatives, distribute them through paid channels and niche social accounts, and use performance data to learn what works, scale winners, and improve every campaign.
one_liner: Scale marketing campaigns across hundreds of personas

name: RentAHuman
one_liner: Marketplace for AI agents to hire humans.

name: Andco
one_liner: End-to-end case workups for personal injury firms.

name: Arga Labs
long_description:
We build environments to test your app and agents against the real world.

Deploy your code/agent into a sandbox that runs against replicas of external services (Stripe, Slack, Google Drive, etc.). Twins support the same APIs, MCP tool calls & SDKs as the real software, and you can spin up thousands of instances in parallel and seed them using natural language. Run real workflows, surface edge cases, and observe how your code/agents actually behave before hitting production.
one_liner: Real-world sandboxes to test agents and agent-facing software

name: Drip
long_description: dripos sends first messages, handles replies, follows up, and books calls across iMessage, LinkedIn, Gmail, Slack, and group chats. You choose who it can message and when it can act.
one_liner: AI that books meetings for you

name: Synphony
long_description:
Strawberry farming is a $3B industry in California alone with 60% labor cost using 40-year old software. Synphony provides strawberry picking robots and software services including: bed-level analytics, data pipeline integration, and agentic solutions.

Synphony's "side hustle" is selling data, evals, and viral demos to robot foundation labs while building capacity for general robot deployments. We've started with strawberry farming for great margins and outdoor data moat, but also received requests for robots in semi-conductors and autonomous space labs. 

Synphony's team has won over 15 hackathons together (taking home 4 Jensen-signed GPUs!). Sean did ML research at NVIDIA and led AI products used by IRS and Citibank. Lucas' research spanned robotics, IoT devices, and neuromorphic computing. Alex architected an app with 400k users.
one_liner: robots & software for farms

name: Callab AI
long_description:
58% of the $400B call center industry runs on on-prem phone systems, and until now bringing AI voice automation to them meant ripping out their entire infrastructure first. That's the largest unserved segment in the market. Callab AI connects directly to legacy PBX systems like Avaya, Cisco UCM, and Mitel, and automates up to 70% of call volume with zero migration needed.

We plug into these environments without requiring a rip-and-replace. We serve clients across three continents, including brands like Dunkin Donuts.
one_liner: AI voice agents for on-prem telephony stack.

name: Pairio
long_description: Pairo is an AI mobile app to help technicians fix broken equipment in factories. Users just take a picture of the broken machine and we search thousands of pages of manuals to find the right solution.
one_liner: AI-native industrial maintenance software

name: Taiga
long_description:
Taiga automates medical billing for doctor's offices. A doctor uploads their visit data, our AI figures out the right billing codes, checks the claim for errors, and submits it to insurance, dealing with any denials. Faster billing, fewer rejected claims, less busywork.                                                       
                                                                                                                        
We're Nanda and Adam, friends since 3rd grade. Both of our parents are doctors with their own practices, and billing was always a source of stress and lost revenue for them. We want providers to treat patients while we handle the rest.
one_liner: Autonomous medical billing

name: StableBrowse
long_description: We are making the browser layer for your AI agents. Agents don’t need to see UIs like us humans do. We give agents a semantic understanding of the web instead of brittle visual interfaces. Your agents can perform deep research, large-scale scraping, and complex automation—more efficiently with our knowledge graphs that turns the web into a native protocol for LLMs.
one_liner: Browser Layer for AI agents

name: Kimpton AI
long_description:
Kimpton is Cursor for portfolio managers, an AI-native terminal where agents do the research and propose the trades. 

It was built by two ex-Goldman engineers and an ex-Vistra Energy engineer who left to start a quantitative systematic hedge fund with $10M raised from a family office at the ages of 21 and 22 years old. They scaled it to $35M at peak over four years, and productized the technology stack they ran it on. 

Portfolio managers spend most of their day on work that isn't investing: filings, earnings prep, 13Fs, peer benchmarking, thesis validation, trade writeups. 

Kimpton runs all of it autonomously and delivers structured Trade Proposals with full rationale, written as diffs to the portfolio and fitted to each manager's mandate and strategy. 

It is built to disagree. Most AI flatters the user, and consensus is poison for investment decisions. Kimpton is grounded in the firm's own mandate, thesis, and positions, so it surfaces breaches and risks the PM would otherwise miss instead of telling them what they want to hear. Risk profile, style, and constraints live in plain-text mandate.md and strategy.md files that drive every analysis the system performs. 

The rest of the desk sits alongside: Deep Research cited to source, natural-language Dashboards, Agentic Charting and Backtests, scheduled Skills for recurring workflows, and auto-generated Reports. Coverage includes FactSet pricing, fundamentals, estimates, transcripts, ownership, and M&A data, plus live prediction markets from Polymarket. 

Humans make the decisions. AI does everything else.
one_liner: AI-Native Investment Research

name: Lamina Labs
long_description: Lamina Labs is building AI infrastructure for instant explainer videos. Simi turns a single prompt into a complete whiteboard video in seconds, helping teams explain ideas, products, workflows, and lessons faster than ever before.
one_liner: AI infrastructure for instant explainer videos at scale

name: BentoLabs AI
long_description:
BentoLabs is the monitoring and learning layer for long-running agents. We detect when agents silently fail or drift from the user's goal, system prompt, or tool contracts, show affected users and root cause, and suggest the prompt, skill, or harness fix.

As more teams deploy agents, keeping them reliable in production becomes mission-critical. Bento sits directly in the production loop and gives teams the operational leverage required to scale agent ecosystems without scaling human firefighting alongside them. The result is a system that turns opaque agents into agents that can be monitored, debugged, and improved continuously.

The founders learned this problem at Emergent (YC S24), where they built and operated production coding agents used by 5M+ users. Abhinav was hire #1 and helped Emergent hit SWE-Bench #1 and scale from $0 to $100M ARR in just 8 months. Kaushik was hire #2, led full-stack engineering at Emergent, and was key to building the infrastructure that made production agents reliable, observable, and debuggable. Bento's self-learning engine has also lifted ARC-AGI-3 (internal) by 2.6x and Terminal-Bench 2.0 (internal) from 42.2% to 52.4% pass@1 with the same model, tools, and budget.
one_liner: Monitoring and learning layer for long-running agents

name: Lab0
long_description:
Enterprise software implementation is broken,  and it has been for decades. 
Every rollout, whether it's a traditional ERP or a new AI system, requires months of manual integration, configuration, and tuning done by armies of system integrators and forward-deployed engineers. 
Lab0 builds agentic systems that automate this layer, compressing months-long implementations into weeks.
one_liner: Automated forward-deployed engineers (AI FDEs)

name: Gojiberry AI
long_description:
Gojiberry is building the AI GTM team for B2B companies.

We start with outbound: Gojiberry finds buyers showing intent, enriches them, reaches out, follows up, handles replies, and books qualified meetings.

Companies use Gojiberry to replace the old way of doing outbound: buying tools, hiring SDRs & agencies, testing lists, writing messages, and guessing what works. Gojiberry turns it into one AI team that finds buyers and books meetings.

Every campaign makes Gojiberry smarter. It learns which buyers convert, which signals matter, which messages get replies, and which actions create revenue. Over time, Gojiberry uses what works across similar companies and industries to help every new customer get better results from day one.

Long term, Gojiberry will become the system that finds every revenue opportunity and acts on it: outbound leads, website visitors, inbound leads, old CRM leads, replies, meetings, CRM updates, new campaign ideas, and account expansion.

The goal is to make every B2B company able to run a high-performing GTM team without hiring one.

We grew from $0 to $2.5M ARR in 10 months, are growing 30% MoM, profitable, and used by 2,000+ customers.

Customers get 2-5x more positive replies than traditional outbound.
one_liner: AI GTM agents for B2B teams

name: Napkin Math
long_description:
Existing consumer health apps focus on scaling a one-size-fits-all solution: calorie deficits. But they don’t answer the questions you actually have.

Food is deeply personal, and so is Napkin Math:

Get the fuel you need for marathon training, while understanding you eat catered office lunches five days a week. Discover what’s triggering your stomach irritation, without sacrificing your weekly dinner with friends. Napkin Math tailors a plan for not only your specific health goal, but also your life.
one_liner: The personalized AI food journal that people love to use.

name: Ontora
long_description: Ontora is an AI Agent that interviews every employee to map how work actually gets done and identify bottlenecks. Consultants spend months doing this manually; Ontora does it in hours.
one_liner: AI agents that interview every employee to map how work gets done.

name: Oddpool
long_description:
Oddpool is a platform that helps quant funds and trading desks harness the power of prediction markets. We make it easy to access, search, and analyze every venue from one place.

Avi and Ritesh have been roommates since freshman year at Georgia Tech. Avi has a Master's in ML and saved Microsoft $6M/year by building a more accurate model on half the hardware. Ritesh has a Master's in Distributed Systems and made buy-side trading systems at Bloomberg 40% faster.

We started trading prediction markets ourselves but couldn't backtest our strategies because the tooling for this emerging asset class didn't exist. The deeper we went, the more we saw that fragmentation between prediction markets and lack of clean data was going to be a big problem.

We launched a prosumer tier and grew to several thousand dollars in MRR within months. Now we're bringing it to institutions, building tooling with the rigor and depth serious desks demand. 

They're the fastest growing asset class in finance, and CFTC applications for new exchanges keep climbing. Each new venue makes the market bigger, the fragmentation worse, and the unified data layer more valuable. It doesn't exist yet. We're building it.
one_liner: Institutional infrastructure for prediction markets

name: Voquill
long_description:
Voquill is the modern AI-native operating system for medical labs. This software manages the entire workflow of the lab (accessioning, workflow, reporting, coding, compliance, and revenue) and contains modern agentic AI tools to help each step. For example, pathologists can write reports 20% faster through our voice AI coworker.

Incumbent lab operating systems are old, don't scale with how modern labs work, and can’t integrate existing AI solutions. As a result, labs are either building their own or paying millions for band-aid solutions and consultants. We're building an agentic platform to manage every aspect of the lab and displace the billions spent on legacy management systems.
one_liner: The modern AI-native operating system for medical labs.

name: Keyframe Labs
long_description: Our real-time, generative models turn AI into lifelike video calls. Deploy in minutes with a few lines of embed code. Photorealism, emotion, and scale for just $0.06 per minute.
one_liner: Turn agents into lifelike video calls. $0.06 a minute.

name: Adialante
long_description:
We know how to improve cancer outcomes by 8x-10x: you catch it early. 

MRI is the most robust and powerful cancer-detection tool in the world, yet systems cost millions to purchase, take months to install, and require high levels of technical skill to operate. We've redesigned MRI from the ground up with new physics, algorithms, and hardware, all to bring cancer screening to the entire world. 

Our mobile MRI is an imaging clinic on wheels. No equipment to buy, no infrastructure needed. For a flat per-scan fee, any medical site can offer MRI cancer screening. Starting with prostate cancer. Then everything else.
one_liner: Cancer screening without barriers

name: Lumius
long_description:
Ultrasound today is still mostly 2D. Clinicians have to imagine the 3D anatomy in their head while also moving the probe at the same time. This makes procedures like vascular access hard to learn and perform consistently, especially for beginners. ~50% fail on their first attempt. Hospitals also spend millions each year on 2D ultrasound training. Meanwhile, existing 3D ultrasound devices can cost >10× as much.

Lumius is building fast, smart, and accessible 3D ultrasound systems to eliminate this 2D guesswork. Our technology shows a clear, real-time 3D view of what’s inside the body. It works over a large volume, not just a thin slice. The device is also affordable, compact, and portable.

We are starting with vascular access, especially central line. Future use cases include blood clot detection, tumor diagnosis, and biopsy guidance. Our long-term vision is a universal 3D ultrasound platform that brings easy-to-use, real-time imaging to a wide range of clinical and eventually consumer applications.
one_liner: Fast, smart, accessible 3D ultrasound for everyone

name: Prototyping.io
long_description: Prototyping.io turns CAD designs into real mechanical parts through an AI-driven manufacturing system. We analyze designs for manufacturability and automate production workflows to deliver high-quality parts faster and at lower cost.
one_liner: Autonomous manufacturing for mechanical parts

name: Sidekick
long_description: Sidekick is an AI-powered SMS assistant for frontline workers in manufacturing, auto dealerships, and retail. The idea came from Justin's father, who owns a manufacturing facility and asked him to build a tool to help with worker onboarding, training, and organizing company knowledge. Workers text questions in any language and get instant answers from their company's documents — no app, no login, works on any phone. They can send photos of equipment for instant identification or voice memos when their hands are dirty or they're wearing gloves. Managers upload documents and record video walkthroughs; Sidekick automatically generates step-by-step tutorials and a searchable knowledge base. When Sidekick can't answer, it escalates to the manager via SMS and learns from the response, getting smarter with every interaction.
one_liner: AI-powered SMS assistant for deskless workers.

name: Result
long_description: Result is where the next generation of founders start and run their companies. In one place, you incorporate, open accounts, take payments, launch a product, and run the operational work of the business: marketing campaigns, customer support, content production, and growth experiments, with agents inside Result helping the founder do the work.
one_liner: Where new internet entrepreneurs build and run their companies

name: Parrot
long_description: Parrot is an agentic OS that understands your business so it can call, negotiate, and coordinate with real humans.
one_liner: Agentic operating system for auto repair shops

name: Mount
long_description:
TLDR: Mount is building the AI insurance carrier, starting with liability coverage for deployed AI agents. As everyone moves AI Agents into production and gives them more responsibility, we help businesses insure and secure them.

Whenever responsibility shifts from human operators to autonomous systems, so too does the liability that accompanies it. The systems also introduce new risks: agentic software failures, prompt injection attacks, autonomous workflow errors, data leaks, and more.

Legacy insurance was not built for this. Existing policies are often silent, ambiguous, or exclude autonomous software, and traditional insurers lack the data and technical capability to assess deployed AI Systems.

Our first product, the Mount AI Liability Policy, is built for mid-market and enterprise companies deploying autonomous AI agents in real business processes. We scan and red-team AI deployments, quantify operational risk, help fix vulnerabilities, and insure the residual risk with purpose-built AI liability coverage.

Mount is best thought of as a highly automated version of today's commercial insurance carriers, which run on manual workflows, layers of intermediaries, and tens of thousands of employees. AI enables automation of underwriting, pricing, policy issuance, claims, fraud detection, customer support, and distribution. We believe that the best way to use this opportunity is to rebuild those processes from the ground up.
one_liner: The AI Insurance Carrier

name: Infera
long_description: Describe an experiment in plain English, and Infera turns it into a validated, instrument-ready run across the equipment your lab already uses. Infera handles the protocol logic, vendor-specific scripts, data, inventory, and institutional knowledge in one system. An AI-native compiler for the lab: one system from intent to execution.
one_liner: Control lab instruments with natural language.

name: AICE
long_description: We are building the next generation of underwater defense systems: autonomous swarms of modular marine drones to secure naval assets and coastal infrastructure against evolving underwater threats.
one_liner: Submarine drones for defense

name: Complir
long_description: AI-powered product compliance platform that makes it easy for retailers to launch and scale products across borders without drowning in regulatory work. Complir's AI agents handle the regulations and documentation across every market you sell in - and keep your entire catalogue audit-ready.
one_liner: Vanta for physical products

name: Alchemize
long_description: Alchemize is building an AI-native customs brokerage. Importers are currently hindered by legacy brokers relying on manual workflows, where hand-keyed entries and slow research result in preventable shipping delays, lack of visibility, and unnecessary costs. Alchemize automates these workflows, delivering expert-level compliance at software speed.
one_liner: AI-Native Customs Brokerage

name: Harbor
long_description:
Harbor is building an AI-native contract research organization (CRO) for clinical trials. We started with an AI-native system of record for clinical trial data (electronic data capture system), grew the software business to seven trials and ~$187k in contracted software revenue over the next twelve months, then expanded into full-service trial execution and signed our first CRO contract worth $1.93M over three years.

Clinical trials consume 45% of all new drug and device development spend. While some of that goes to software, sites, and patients, the largest line items are services: project management, monitoring, data management, site coordination, source verification, query resolution, record reconciliation, and analysis.

Today, drug and device companies outsource clinical operations to legacy CROs and their massive teams, which is why the CRO market is $82B annually. Harbor delivers the regulated services companies buy from legacy CROs, but built around our AI-native system of record from day one. Our software already automates the core data workflows: study build, data capture, query generation, monitoring, signing, and database lock. Because we own the system of record, we can expand into the surrounding service workflows: site contracting and billing, subject engagement and retention, statistics, reporting, and more.

We built Harbor because we lived through the problem. Albert spent four years in clinical trials and regulatory strategy at a medical device startup, taking an unproven wearable from first-in-human trials to FDA authorization. Nate brings the technical depth, having built scalable software at Google and Ramp and served as employee #1 at a previous YC startup.
one_liner: AI-native CRO for clinical trial operations

name: Allowance
long_description:
Allowance lets AI agents make purchases on a user’s behalf without ever exposing your real card number.

Users approve purchases directly from their iPhone with customizable controls like spending limits, merchant restrictions, and expiration windows. Once approved, Allowance generates scoped, one-time payment credentials designed specifically for that transaction.

This enables AI agents to safely complete tasks like shopping, ordering food, booking reservations, or purchasing tickets while keeping humans in control of every payment.
one_liner: Scoped payment credentials for AI agents

name: smol machines
long_description:
Smol machines solve the problem of "but app works on my machine" by shipping the machine.

Smol machine enables devs to ship a lightweight virtual machine with any software, similar to how Electron ships an entire browser with the web app.
one_liner: Ship software faster with portable, self contained virtual machines.

name: River Markets
long_description: River Markets is the prime brokerage for prediction markets. We aggregate every major event-contract venue into one account, giving professional traders best execution, portfolio and order management systems, and a unified API.
one_liner: Prime brokerage for prediction markets

name: Archer
long_description:
Archer is the agentic commerce platform for customer acquisition and retention.

We help companies deploy AI agents that can automatically purchase, personalize, and deliver physical gifts on behalf of their business. Instead of manually coordinating rewards, swag, meals, experiences, and gifts, companies can give agents budgets, goals, and instructions, allowing them to take action automatically when important business events occur.

Today, businesses use Archer to automate corporate gifting across outbound sales, customer onboarding, referrals, partnerships, customer success, and employee milestones. A prospect books a meeting. A customer completes onboarding. A partner generates a referral. An agent understands the context, selects an appropriate gift, purchases it, and handles fulfillment automatically.

What makes Archer different is that companies no longer need to manage gifting campaigns manually. Agents continuously monitor what is happening across a business and execute personalized physical experiences at the right moment for the right person.

We believe corporate gifting is just the beginning. As AI agents become increasingly capable of understanding business context through CRMs, product data, Slack, Linear, Notion, and other systems, they will become active participants in how companies build relationships, acquire customers, retain users, and grow revenue.

For decades, businesses have relied on software to track relationships. We believe the next generation of businesses will rely on AI agents to strengthen them.
one_liner: AI-powered gifting for customer acquisition and retention

name: Minicor
long_description:
Minicor connects AI startups to legacy desktop apps that have no API. Reliably, quickly and at scale.

Describe a workflow and get an API endpoint. Each call triggers a self-healing desktop automation on a Windows VM. On-prem, cloud, or Citrix. Build through your coding agent via our MCP.

Complete with the observability tools to manage your customer relationship: full video replay of every automation, granular logging, and slack enabled alerts.
one_liner: RPA platform for deploying AI into legacy desktop systems

name: Datost
long_description: Datost watches your docs, Slack, databases, data lakes, and codebase, surfacing issues, answering questions, and flagging anomalies before anyone asks. Query, debug, and analyze right where your team works
one_liner: The proactive AI data analyst

name: tday.com
long_description: tday automatically turns a software company's product into on-brand marketing creative. Connect your product and git repo, and tday generates polished launch videos, social graphics, and ads, the content a team would normally spend hours making in a design tool or pay a freelancer for. tday then publishes the creative and measures how it performs across your social and ad channels, closing the loop from what you ship to what you post.
one_liner: AI that turns your product into on-brand marketing content

name: General Instinct
one_liner: Deployable Physical AI on Any Edge Device.

name: ValCtrl
long_description:
ValCtrl is building the world model for Prediction Finance. Prediction markets contain useful prices about the future, but today those prices are fragmented across venues, titles, rules, and proxy contracts. Users think in beliefs; markets are stored as scattered contracts.

Our first product is Kassandre Search. Users type a natural-language belief, and Kassandre maps it to relevant prediction markets with backtrace and quality labels. Existing markets are the first scaffold. The long-term product path is search -> composed exposures -> native quotes -> gated bet/trade placement, with legal/compliance gates respected at each step.

We are looking for hard belief-to-market queries from forecasters, investors, operators, and YC founders.
one_liner: ValCtrl is the world model for Prediction Finance, and Kassandre…

name: Ara
long_description: Ara is the self-driving IDE for software teams. It watches work in progress, suggests the next step, and executes tasks through non-invasive computer use, background agents, memory, and reusable skills. Ara turns coding into a closed loop: suggest, act, learn, improve.
one_liner: Ara is the self-driving IDE for software teams.

name: Zibra Labs
former_names: TBD. We don't have a name yet.
long_description:
We build distributed compute clusters with the cheapest CPUs and GPUs across Hyperscalers and Neoclouds for AI. Our mission is to bring frontier-grade infrastructure to everyone.

We're starting by building large scale high performance computing (HPC) clusters for quantitative trading firms to run parallel simulation workloads such as backtesting. 

Our technology generalizes to critical AI workloads such as post-training with reinforcement learning, fine-tuning, long-horizon agents with high tool use, and batch inference.
one_liner: Distributed Compute for AI

name: PLAN0 AI
long_description:
PLAN0 is building the Bloomberg of construction for real estate developers, investors, and general contractors — $20B of projects are already running through the platform.

We analyze architectural plans using vision models to generate detailed cost estimates and analytics in minutes. Through large scale industry partnerships, the platform also aggregates historical and real-time project data across all major geographies to power ML-driven analytics, decomposing costs into market-observable factors and predicting local trade-level pricing up to 24 months out.
one_liner: AI native construction cost estimation & analytics.

name: Armature
long_description:
Armature helps companies monitor, analyze, and optimize how AI agents experience their products.

What we do today:
- Automated testing: We run complex sessions against your MCPs/CLIs across all major harnesses and models to catch broken workflows before your users do.
- AX analytics & benchmarking: No more black boxes. We trace agent sessions to show you exactly what the model "thought", what context it missed, and why it failed. We measure your AX against competitors to ensure you remain the preferred platform for agents to use.
- Auto-remediation: Our MCP lets your internal coding agents plug into Armature to autonomously fix your product's AX based on our run data.

Our vision for tomorrow:
Reaching full understanding of how agents use your product and how you can improve it is only step one. 
The next step is to provide the foundational infrastructure for the Agent Experience, powering agent-native revenue, lifecycle marketing, and customer support for the next generation of software.
one_liner: Armature helps companies monitor and optimize the agent experience.

name: Soria
long_description: Soria is an AI-native Bloomberg Terminal for investors who specialize in a particular sector, starting with healthcare. Our agents convert hundreds of the most important public and private sources into an always-on financial model, reviewed by domain experts and delivered to hedge funds, banks, and asset managers through a terminal UI, MCP, API, warehouse sync, and research feed. Unlike our competitors, we own the data layer and expand bottom-up, one sector at a time - additionally our agents proactively do research on behalf of our clients without them lifting a finger or sharing any data.
one_liner: AI Bloomberg Terminal built for Healthcare (and beyond)

name: Aseon Labs
long_description:
Aseon Labs builds robotic pitstops for self-driving cars. A depot in a box for charging, cleaning & inspecting autonomous fleets directly in operating zones.

Autonomous driving is working. The operational layer around it is not. Today, fleets lose significant time and money traveling to centralized depots for charging, cleaning and servicing, creating dead miles, lower uptime and operational bottlenecks that limit fleet scale.

Aseon Pods are modular, rapidly deployable robotic pitstops that keep autonomous fleets operating near demand. By bringing charging, cleaning and inspection directly into operating zones, we help fleet operators increase uptime, reduce operational overhead and scale markets more efficiently without relying on large centralized depots.

We’re second-time founders and mobility infrastructure operators. Previously, we built and scaled Pushme to 5,000 stations across 40 cities before its acquisition by TIER–Dott ($600M raised). Aseon Labs is backed by Y Combinator and headquartered in Redwood City.
one_liner: Robotic pitstops for self-driving cars

name: Walter
long_description: Walter is an AI employee for manufacturing operations.
one_liner: AI Employee for Manufacturing Operations

name: Chronicle Labs
long_description: Chronicle Labs is a staging environment for enterprise AI agents: we capture every event the agent sees in production and backtest it, so customers can safely test new behaviors without breaking anything.
one_liner: Staging Environments for Enterprise AI Agents

name: Tasklet
long_description:
AI chatbots can answer questions, but they can't do your work — they aren't connected to your tools, can't take actions, and stop running when you close the tab. Meanwhile, knowledge workers are still spending most of their day switching between dozens of SaaS apps, manually stitching together workflows that should be automated.

Tasklet is the AI agent that actually does the work. It connects to every tool you use — via thousands of built-in integrations, any HTTP API, or MCP servers — and takes real actions on your behalf: pulling reports, updating CRMs, triaging emails, processing data, and more. When it needs a UI, it generates one on the fly. When it needs to crunch numbers or do bulk operations, it writes and runs code in a cloud sandbox. When it needs to interact with a website that doesn't have an API, it uses a real browser. And because it runs in the cloud 24/7, you can set up triggers to have it take ongoing responsibility for workflows — not just do something once, but handle it going forward.

The product is deliberately simple: a chat interface where you talk to an extremely capable agent. But underneath that simplicity is a cloud-native agent runtime, best-in-class context engineering, a universal connections layer, and multi-model support that keeps us on the frontier as AI improves. 

We think legacy SaaS is being unbundled by agents, and Tasklet is the operating system that ties it all together.
one_liner: The cloud agent operating system for knowledge work

name: Wato
long_description: Wato gives AI agents shared memory, reusable workflows, connected tools, and living artifacts so work compounds across a team instead of disappearing into chat threads. Teams use Wato to give every agent access to trusted company context and turn useful runs into knowledge the next agent can build on.
one_liner: Shared memory, tools, and workflows for agents across teams

name: Kuli
long_description:
Kuli is an AI coworker for marketing teams at the world's largest consumer brands. Our agent watches all videos across social media, learns the brand's context and helps them turn signals from billions of social videos into winning campaigns without increasing headcount.

Previous platforms gave marketers databases to search through and a CRM. Kuli actually watches the content. Marketers at team like Disney, Nivea and Havas use it to scout creators, run analysis on trends and competitor strategies, and analyse what makes their campaign perform better.

For 10 years, influencer marketing meant more tools, more tabs, more spreadsheets. Kuli replaces all of that with one single agent you orchestrate. One marketer with Kuli does what a $50K/month agency bills for manual tasks.
one_liner: AI coworker for marketing teams at the largest consumer brands

name: Ardent
long_description: Clone any postgres DB regardless of size in 6s so agents can test their work

name: BioStack Platforms
long_description:
BioStack is building the data engine for healthcare and drug discovery AI. The bottleneck is not models. It is access to high-quality biological data. Clinical and experimental data is fragmented, unstructured, and locked inside hospitals, labs, and CROs, while generating new data is slow and expensive. BioStack fixes this with proprietary clinical and preclinical data pipelines that turn real biomedical workflows into ML-ready training environments.

We structure longitudinal multimodal data across imaging, EHR, and experimental assays, then package it for post-training and reinforcement learning so models can learn how research and care actually happen. Instead of static datasets, BioStack gives AI labs workflow-aligned data and environments that improve reasoning, decision-making, and real-world performance in biology and medicine.
one_liner: Real world training envs for healthcare AI models

name: Silmaril
long_description: Silmaril is self-healing prompt injection defense for AI native applications and agents. It understands application context to block 2x as many threats as current SOTA defenses with 10x lower latency. Customers plug us into agentic frameworks like LangGraph with 5 lines of code. Silmaril has stopped $28M of damages for customers.
